\chapter{Table T4 Validation Protocols (Full)}
\label{ch:app_d_validation}

Pre-registered validation machinery for Part V Hypotheses. Chapter 10 keeps a short summary; this appendix is the full checklist, hypothesis catalog, and demo usage.

Helpers: \texttt{lib/validation.py}.

\bigskip
\noindent\rule{\textwidth}{0.4pt}
\bigskip

\section{D.1 Core checklist (Table T4)}
\label{ch:app_d_validation:d-1-core-checklist-table-t4}

\begin{center}
\small
\begin{tabular}{@{}lp{0.28\textwidth}p{0.28\textwidth}@{}}
\toprule
ID & Element & Description \\
\midrule
T4.1 & Null hypothesis definition & State \(H_0\) with significance level \(\alpha\) (default \(0.01\)). \\
T4.2 & Data sources and preprocessing & List datasets, windows, and cleaning rules. \\
T4.3 & Test statistic & Define the scalar or vector statistic used for decision. \\
T4.4 & Multiple-testing correction & Bonferroni or FDR control across domains/tests. \\
T4.5 & Power analysis & Minimum detectable effect at 80\% power. \\
T4.6 & Falsification criteria & What result counts as strong evidence against the alternative. \\
T4.7 & Pre-registration & Timestamped commit or external registry \textbf{before} looking at new data. \\
T4.8 & Reproducibility & Full code, seeds, and environment for every figure and table. \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}


\begin{lstlisting}[style=qga,language=python]
from lib.validation import table_t4_checklist
for row in table_t4_checklist():
    print(row["id"], row["element"])
\end{lstlisting}

\bigskip
\noindent\rule{\textwidth}{0.4pt}

\section{D.2 Catalog of major hypotheses}
\label{ch:app_d_validation:d-2-catalog-of-major-hypotheses}

These match \texttt{default\_hypotheses()} in code.

\subsection{H1 --- Multi-domain \(350/\pi\)}
\label{ch:app_d_validation:h1-multi-domain-350-pi}

\begin{center}
\small
\begin{tabular}{@{}lp{0.55\textwidth}@{}}
\toprule
Field & Content \\
\midrule
\textbf{Claim} & \(W_g=350/\pi\) is a shared topological clock across named domains. \\
\textbf{Type} & Hypothesis \\
\textbf{Null} & Recurrence near \(350/\pi\) is consistent with random coincidence at \(\alpha=0.01\). \\
\textbf{Domains} & pulsar timing; Bitcoin Pi Cycle; TLS trees; cuprate SC sketches; structural constants \\
\textbf{Sources} & \texttt{kingdom/observations/}; portal Observations tab; related assets \\
\textbf{Statistic} & Combined \(p\)-value or topological distance to model sequences \\
\textbf{Correction} & Bonferroni \\
\textbf{Falsification} & Fail to reject \(H_0\) after pre-registration + correction; or independent replication finds no excess clustering \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}


\subsection{H2 --- \(Z\mapsto\) map as periodic-table proxy}
\label{ch:app_d_validation:h2-z-mapsto-map-as-periodic-table-proxy}

\begin{center}
\small
\begin{tabular}{@{}lp{0.55\textwidth}@{}}
\toprule
Field & Content \\
\midrule
\textbf{Claim} & \texttt{map\_z\_to\_flywheel} stability peaks reflect genuine chemical/nuclear specialness beyond model tuning. \\
\textbf{Type} & Hypothesis \\
\textbf{Null} & High scores near noble gases are explained by explicit model bonuses alone (no extra predictive content). \\
\textbf{Domains} & periodic table; ionization energy; nuclear magic numbers \\
\textbf{Statistic} & Out-of-sample correlation or ablation study \\
\textbf{Falsification} & Ablating noble-gas bonuses removes all predictive alignment; held-out properties not above chance \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}


\subsection{H3 --- Magic Island \(\leftrightarrow\) class-number association}
\label{ch:app_d_validation:h3-magic-island-class-number-association}

\begin{center}
\small
\begin{tabular}{@{}lp{0.55\textwidth}@{}}
\toprule
Field & Content \\
\midrule
\textbf{Claim} & Magic Island structure is predicted by class-number-like invariants. \\
\textbf{Type} & Hypothesis \\
\textbf{Null} & Island locations independent of \texttt{class\_number\_analogue}. \\
\textbf{Statistic} & Association / rank correlation \\
\textbf{Correction} & FDR \\
\textbf{Falsification} & No significant association after pre-registration \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}


\begin{lstlisting}[style=qga,language=python]
from lib.validation import default_hypotheses
for h in default_hypotheses():
    print(h.name, h.alpha, h.null_hypothesis[:60], "...")
\end{lstlisting}

\section{D.3 Statistical helpers}
\label{ch:app_d_validation:d-3-statistical-helpers}

\begin{lstlisting}[style=qga,language=python]
from lib.validation import (
    WG_350_OVER_PI,
    bonferroni_threshold,
    combine_p_values_fisher,
    proximity_to_wg,
    run_table_t4_demo,
    toy_multidomain_pvalues,
)

print("W_g =", WG_350_OVER_PI)
print("Bonferroni thr (?=0.01, n=5):", bonferroni_threshold(0.01, 5))

# Toy null demo -- NOT evidence for 350/?
demo = run_table_t4_demo(seed=1, alpha=0.01)
print(demo["decision"], demo["fisher"])

# Diagnostic closeness of constants to W_g
print(proximity_to_wg([111.4, 111.5, 110.0, 111.408]))
\end{lstlisting}

Under the \textbf{null toy} generator, expect frequent \texttt{fail\_to\_reject\_H0}. Real domain \(p\)-values without pre-registration do \textbf{not} count as T4 success.

\bigskip
\noindent\rule{\textwidth}{0.4pt}
\bigskip

\section{D.4 Decision flowchart (narrative)}
\label{ch:app_d_validation:d-4-decision-flowchart-narrative}

\begin{enumerate}
\item Write \(H_0\), \(\alpha\), statistic, and falsification (T4.1, T4.3, T4.6).  
\item Lock data sources and preprocessing (T4.2).  
\item Pre-register (T4.7) --- e.g. git tag / commit hash.  
\item Compute statistics; apply multiple-testing correction (T4.4).  
\item Check power (T4.5).  
\item Decide: reject \(H_0\) / fail to reject / refine design.  
\item Publish code and seeds (T4.8).
\end{enumerate}

Figure 10.4 in Chapter 10 is the visual summary of this flow.

\bigskip
\noindent\rule{\textwidth}{0.4pt}
\bigskip

\section{D.5 Claim discipline for validation results}
\label{ch:app_d_validation:d-5-claim-discipline-for-validation-results}

\begin{center}
\small
\begin{tabular}{@{}lp{0.55\textwidth}@{}}
\toprule
Statement & Label \\
\midrule
``Table T4 exists in the repo'' & \textbf{Software fact} \\
``Hypothesis \(X\) passed T4'' & \textbf{Hypothesis} until full checklist executed and reviewed \\
``\(W_g\) is a law of nature'' & \textbf{Hypothesis} (not default) \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}
